source.description: Built-in image generation created a six-panel marker process contact sheet for the skateboard-sticker-doodle lesson.
source.notes: The contact sheet was reviewed before cropping; the final panel is used as the finished asset.

process_strategy: The six frames keep one front-facing skateboard sticker pose, introducing the deck, trucks, wheels, face, sticker shapes, and marker colors before the final outline pass.

final_elements.name: rounded skateboard deck
final_elements.description: A rounded skateboard deck forms the main sticker silhouette.

final_elements.name: trucks and wheels
final_elements.description: Two truck bars and four small wheels sit under the board.

final_elements.name: cute face
final_elements.description: Simple eyes and a smile give the board a playful expression.

final_elements.name: star and stripe stickers
final_elements.description: A small star and diagonal stripes decorate the board face.

final_elements.name: thick black outline
final_elements.description: A bold black marker outline wraps the deck, trucks, wheels, and decorations.

final_elements.name: bright marker fills
final_elements.description: Teal, yellow, red, and gray marker fills add saturated sticker color.

frames.visible_job: Block in the rounded skateboard deck with light marker lines.
Place the two truck bars and four small wheels under the deck.
Add the simple eyes and smile centered on the board.
Add the star and diagonal stripe decorations without changing the deck shape.
Thicken the black outline and add teal, yellow, red, and gray marker fills.

final_step.visible_job: Retrace the existing deck and wheel edges, even out the marker fills, and add tiny highlights only to existing shapes.

rejection_checks: No major contour, prop, marking, or color choice should first appear in the final image.
No adjacent frame should look unchanged at card size.
The final image must keep the same pose, scale, and placement as the step frames.
